data.debug_attr_logging: Debug: log all attribute values to CSV
data.debug_raw_logging: Debug: log raw BLE packets to CSV

data_description.device_key: 10-character hex key unique to your device (e.g. bd236b1695). You can override it here if needed.
data_description.continuous_connection: Keep the Bluetooth connection open permanently instead of polling every 30 seconds. Captures infrequent sensor updates (e.g. battery voltage) but blocks the official app from connecting at the same time.
data_description.poll_interval: How often (in seconds) to connect and pull sensor data when not using continuous mode. Range: 10–600. Default: 30.
data_description.stale_timeout: How long (in minutes) to keep showing the last known values after a failed poll before marking entities as unavailable. Range: 1–120. Default: 15.
data_description.runtime_max_minutes: Maximum plausible runtime value in minutes. When firmware reports a value above this (e.g. during charging or idle), the sensor is clamped to this value instead of going Unknown. Default: 5940 (99 h). Increase if you have a large device stack capable of genuine runtimes over 99 h.
data_description.debug_attr_logging: Log parsed attribute values with packet classification to a CSV in your HA config folder. Includes direction (TX/RX), command type (cmd1/cmd2_resp/cmd3_resp), packet index, and parsed values.
data_description.debug_raw_logging: Log every raw BLE packet (TX and RX) with direction and command classification to a CSV in your HA config folder. Useful for diagnosing missing settings responses.

user.title: OUPES Mega Power Station
user.description: Enter the Bluetooth MAC address of your OUPES Mega device. The device advertises via BLE as **TT**. If a device was already found nearby it will be pre-filled below.

bluetooth_confirm.title: OUPES Mega Power Station
bluetooth_confirm.description:
Found **{name}** ({address}) nearby.

Device ID: `{device_id}`

Click **Submit** to continue with setup.

choose_method.title: How would you like to provide the device key?
choose_method.description:
Setting up **{name}** ({address}).

Choose how you want to provide the device key needed for BLE communication.

create_key.title: Create New Device Key
create_key.description:
This will pair **{name}** ({address}) with a new device key over Bluetooth.

**Before clicking Submit:**
1. Press and hold the **IoT button** on the device for **5 seconds**
2. Wait until the indicator light **flashes rapidly** (factory reset complete)
3. The IoT module (Wi-Fi/BLE) will turn off — **press the IoT button once to turn it back on**
4. The device is now in pairing mode

A random key has been generated below. You can change it or leave the default. Pairing takes about 20 seconds.

data.go_back: ← Go back and choose a different method

data_description.device_key: A random key has been pre-filled. You can keep it or enter your own 10-character hex key.
data_description.go_back: Check this and click Submit to return to the method selection screen.

existing_key.title: Enter Existing Device Key
existing_key.description:
Enter the 10-character hex device key for **{name}** ({address}).

You can find this key by:
- Running `adb logcat | findstr 8888888888888888888` while opening the Cleanergy app
- Checking a btsnoop/PCAPdroid capture of the Cleanergy app traffic
- Using the Cleanergy cloud API via the PowerShell script in the README

data.go_back: ← Go back and choose a different method

data_description.device_key: 10-character hex key unique to your device (e.g. bd236b1695).
data_description.go_back: Check this and click Submit to return to the method selection screen.

cloud_login.title: Fetch Key from Cleanergy Cloud
cloud_login.description:
Log in with your Cleanergy app credentials to automatically fetch the device key for **{name}** ({address}).

Your credentials are used **once** to retrieve the key and are **not stored**.

⚠️ The Cleanergy API uses unencrypted HTTP — this mirrors the official app's own behaviour.

data.go_back: ← Go back and choose a different method

data_description.cloud_email: The email address you use to log in to the Cleanergy app.
data_description.cloud_password: Your Cleanergy app password. Used once, not stored.
data_description.go_back: Check this and click Submit to return to the method selection screen.

pairing.title: Pairing in progress…
pairing.description:
Pairing with **{name}** ({address}). This takes about 20 seconds.

Please wait…

data.debug_attr_logging: Debug: log parsed attribute values to CSV
data.debug_raw_logging: Debug: log raw BLE packets to CSV
data.go_back: ← Go back and choose a different method

data_description.continuous_connection: Keep the Bluetooth connection open permanently instead of polling. Recommended for reliable sensor data. Prevents the official Cleanergy app from connecting at the same time.
data_description.poll_interval: How often (in seconds) to connect and pull sensor data when not using continuous mode. Range: 10–600. Default: 30.
data_description.stale_timeout: How long (in minutes) to keep showing the last known values after a failed poll before marking entities as unavailable. Range: 1–120. Default: 15.
data_description.runtime_max_minutes: Maximum plausible runtime value in minutes. When firmware reports a value above this (e.g. during charging or idle), the sensor is clamped to this value instead of going Unknown. Default: 5940 (99 h). Increase if you have a large device stack capable of genuine runtimes over 99 h.
data_description.debug_attr_logging: Log parsed attribute values with packet classification to a CSV in your HA config folder. Includes direction (TX/RX), command type, packet index, and parsed values.
data_description.debug_raw_logging: Log every raw BLE packet (TX and RX) with direction and command classification to a CSV in your HA config folder. Useful for diagnosing missing settings responses.
data_description.go_back: Check this and click Submit to return to the method selection screen.

progress.pairing: Pairing with the device over Bluetooth… This takes about 20 seconds.

error.invalid_address: Invalid Bluetooth MAC address format. Use XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX.
error.invalid_device_key: Device key must be exactly 10 hexadecimal characters (e.g. bd236b1695).
error.cloud_login_failed: Cloud login failed. Check your Cleanergy email and password, or go back and try a different method.
error.no_device_key: A device key is required. Go back and choose a method to provide it.
error.pairing_device_not_found: Device not found via Bluetooth. Make sure the device is powered on, in range, and in pairing mode (hold IoT button 5 seconds until rapid flash).
error.pairing_connection_failed: Could not connect to the device. Make sure no other app (e.g. Cleanergy) is connected and the device is in pairing mode.
error.pairing_timeout: Pairing did not complete. Try again — make sure the device is in pairing mode (hold IoT button 5 seconds until rapid flash). The device may need a moment after the reset before it accepts pairing.
